strategy: Hit with Myth (+40% incoming); avoid Storm (70% resist).
Do not rely on Blades. Takanobu Disarms any blade off the receiver. Set up with traps and Feint instead.

The Bladeless Ronin Defuses your traps based on your attack school: a Myth, Life, or Death hit triggers Spirit Defuse, an elemental hit triggers Elemental Defuse. Land your hit the same round you set traps, or kill the Ronin first.
Do not cast Storm, Ice, or Fire shields on yourself; he answers with a Storm Prism on you.
Burst the 4480 health with one funneled Myth hit before he stockpiles pips for Supercharge or Darkwind.
cheatNotes: Casting a Blade gets it Disarmed off the receiver. Use traps, not blades.
A triple blade only loses its last blade to the Disarm.
A multi-target blade only loses the caster's copy.
Casting Storm, Volcanic, or Glacial Shield on yourself makes him put a Storm Prism on you.
If he holds 5+ and an odd number of pips at a round's end, he Supercharges himself (+50%). Deny it by ending the fight fast.
With 6+ pips at the start of a round he casts Darkwind. Pressure him before he stockpiles pips.
Attacking with a Balance spell makes the Ronin cast Unbalance on you (an accuracy debuff).
An AoE Balance attack triggers a separate Unbalance for each enemy hit.
Attacks that deal no direct damage (like Scald or Heck Hound) do not trigger any Ronin cheats.
Attacking with Fire, Ice, or Storm makes the Ronin Elemental Defuse your elemental traps.
An AoE elemental attack triggers one Elemental Defuse per enemy hit.
If a prism converts your hit to Life, Death, or Myth, you get Spirit Defuse instead.
Attacking with Life, Death, or Myth makes the Ronin Spirit Defuse your spirit traps. Hit the same round you trap.
An AoE spirit attack triggers one Spirit Defuse per enemy hit.
Multi-hit attacks trigger a separate Spirit Defuse per hit.
If a prism converts your hit to Fire, Ice, or Storm, you get Elemental Defuse instead.
